Home / Quality Review

Internal Ledger

Quality Review

A working ledger for finding weak reconstructed prompt-response sections and returning to them branch by branch.

Quality Scope

Prompt-response coverage

This review tracks 1785 generated prompt-response sections. Average score is 99.5. Sections needing review: 0 (0.0%). Exceptional gap-fill backlog: 299 (16.8%).

Levels are heuristic, not final judgment: exceptional, strong, good, developing, and thin. “Developing” and “thin” are the backlog for repair; the gap-fill column is the more interesting backlog for adding quotations, source texture, and the kind of detail that keeps philosophy from becoming a very confident table of contents.

Editorial Audit

Scrutiny pass

The strict editorial audit currently finds 0 stale headings, grammar scars, or unsupported asset references. This does not mean the archive is finished; it means the obvious gremlins have stopped waving from the balcony.

Philosophers Gap-Fill

Canon needs encounter, not nameplates

The Philosophers branch is intentionally marked for future enrichment. The next exceptional pass should add primary-source fragments, historical settings, signature problems, influence trails, and objections that let each thinker speak with a recognizable philosophical temperature. Otherwise we risk producing a very elegant hall of portraits, which is lovely, but portraits rarely argue back.

Exceptional Backlog

Good enough is not the ceiling

  • Ethics / Fictional Meta-Ethics Debate / prompt-2Source hierarchy is sparse; later hand-curation should add examples, objections, or context.
  • Philosophers / Aristotle / prompt-1Philosopher page needs eventual primary-source texture, historical setting, and influence trail.
